action: Cycle-270's advisor-inserted bug-reproduction phase wrote source files into the MAIN tree and NO tree-diff guard fired — the cycle proceeded to audit/ship as if clean. Its own report declares the main-tree path as the deliverable (repro.test_path=go/internal/looppreflight/bug_reproduction_test.go) and a matching boot.go edit landed beside it (the pair is a coherent failing-test+fix, salvaged interactively 2026-06-10). The SAME disease correctly FAILed spine phases in cycles 262 (build, leaked .evolve/commit-prefix-scope.json) and 268 (tdd, leaked .evolve/evals/*) — so detection exists but coverage is phase-scoped. Hypothesis: the guard snapshots before each SOURCE-WRITING phase (orchestrator.go ~111 'Workstream B's tree-diff guard snapshots this before each source-writing phase') and advisor-inserted/optional phases (bug-reproduction; likely any minted phase) are not in that set, so their main-tree writes are invisible. Fix direction: key guard coverage on WRITE CAPABILITY, not phase identity — any phase whose profile/contract permits Write/Edit/shell gets the snapshot+boundary-diff; or snapshot once at cycle start and diff at EVERY phase boundary. Acceptance: a test pins that an inserted phase writing outside its worktree FAILs the cycle with leaked paths named; a cycle-270-shaped replay (insert bug-reproduction post-build, write to main tree) is caught at the phase boundary.
evidence_pointer: .evolve/runs/cycle-270/bug-reproduction-report.md (repro.test_path names the main tree); .evolve/validation-batch-adr0044-r7.log (no guard line for the phase; cycle proceeded to audit); contrast carryover entries cycle-262-failed-build / cycle-268-failed-tdd (guard fired for spine phases)
